title: Review and setlist: Leslie Odom Jr. brings Broadway to the Boston Pops
author: Shannon Murphy
bullets: Leslie Odom Jr. performed with the Boston Pops at Symphony Hall on May 22 during their 2026 spring season.
Odom Jr. performed a mix of Broadway hits and classic standards, including a rendition of 'Wait for It' from Hamilton.
The concert celebrated American musical art forms, featuring works by Buck, Copland, Ellington, and a medley performed by Odom Jr.

title: Chelsea man facing slew of assault charges for alleged violence against young children
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Kener Bautista-Zepeda, 19, from Chelsea, is charged with multiple assault counts involving two children under 14.
Allegations include burning a child with a cigarette lighter and another with a heated fork, causing substantial injuries.
Bautista-Zepeda fled to Virginia; he was arrested and awaits a dangerousness hearing set for May 28.

title: Concord man, 73, convicted of raping 20-year-old in Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Elie Jammal, 73, of Concord, convicted of rape and indecent assault in a Sandwich restaurant in 2023.
Jammal sentenced to 13 ½ to 15 years in prison, plus 5 years probation for assault conviction.
Victim's family confronted Jammal, who fled before police arrived; he later faced charges in November 2023.

title: Newton Church, founded in 1893, is becoming condos
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The Church of the Open Word in Newtonville will become six condos, with a new building adding 26 more.
Habitech Communities bought the former church property in 2024 for $4 million, after arts center plans fell through.
The project reflects a trend of converting former churches into residences in Greater Boston due to shrinking congregations.

title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Kawhi Leonard may be available for trade and is considered a strong target for the Celtics if Giannis Antetokounmpo isn't acquired.
LeBron James is an unrestricted free agent and could be an option for the Celtics, despite his likelihood to stay in LA.
John Collins, Jaxson Hayes, and Coby White are other potential targets, providing depth and offensive options for Boston.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advanced to the NCAA Division II World Series after a 3-2 victory over Molloy University in the Super Regionals.
Endicott College defeated Salve Regina to secure its spot in the Division III championship round for the fourth consecutive year.
Bentley and Endicott's World Series schedules and opponents are yet to be determined after these historic achievements.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Celtics will work out UConn forward Alex Karaban next week ahead of the 2026 NBA Draft.
Karaban, a Southborough native, is ranked No. 32 on ESPN's top 100 draft prospects list.
Karaban played four years at UConn, winning two NCAA championships and becoming the program's winningest player.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth delivered a concert combining jazz and pop at MGM Music Hall in Fenway on May 22, 2026.
Puth performed songs from his new album 'Whatever's Clever!' alongside older hits, thrilling the Boston audience.
Openers Ally Salort and Daniel Seavey impressed with indie-pop sounds and live-looping musical versatility.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers performed 'Bringing Down the Horse' and Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark' at The Cabot in Beverly.
Jakob Dylan's gruffer voice suited the sardonic lyrics, enhancing songs like 'Sixth Avenue Heartache' and 'Three Marlenas'.
The encore featured Wallflowers' tracks and Petty classics, concluding with 'Refugee' and 'The Waiting'.

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston saw the largest population decline in Massachusetts, losing 1,338 residents, according to U.S. Census data.
Everett and Worcester experienced the most significant population gains, adding 2,362 and 1,916 residents respectively.
High housing costs and reduced immigration are contributing to population shifts within Massachusetts.
